title: Vectors
summary: Free 9709 Pure Maths 3 vectors — component form, vector addition, magnitude, direction, and the scalar (dot) product with exam-style reasoning.

simpleExplanation.title: Arrows with components
simpleExplanation.summary: Vectors have magnitude and direction. On Paper 3 you add in component form, find length and angle, and use the dot product for angles and projections.
steps: Write each vector in component form using i and j (or a column vector).
Add component-wise: the resultant has components (a₁+b₁, a₂+b₂).
Find |R| = √(Rx² + Ry²) and direction from tan θ = Ry/Rx.
Use a·b = |a||b|cos θ for angles, perpendicularity (a·b = 0), and projections.

sections.content: **Vectors on Paper 3** extend the 2D work from earlier papers. You combine vectors in **component form**, find **magnitude and direction**, and use the **scalar product** for angles between lines — a favourite source of M1/A1 split marks.
a = a₁i + a₂j, b = b₁i + b₂j ⇒ a + b = (a₁+b₁)i + (a₂+b₂)j. Magnitude |a| = √(a₁² + a₂²).

items: Use i, j unit vectors or column notation consistently
Add corresponding components — x with x, y with y
Direction angle: tan θ = a₂/a₁ (watch quadrant)
Scalar product: a·b = a₁b₁ + a₂b₂ = |a||b|cos θ

sections.content: When vectors are **perpendicular**, a·b = 0 — use this to find unknown components. For the angle between two vectors, cos θ = (a·b)/(|a||b|). Show **numerator and denominators** separately before rounding.
Draw a quick diagram for geometry questions — it clarifies which vector is **a − b** vs **b − a**. Keep surd form until the final line unless the paper specifies decimal answers.
